Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_bom. Business justification: The batch_record currently stores bom_version as a denormalized STRING. This should be replaced with a proper FK to manufacturing_bom, which contains the authoritative BOM version, component structure
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_facility. Business justification: Batch record should reference the specific manufacturing facility for traceability.
Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Brand Batch Traceability Report requires linking each batch to its brand for recall, quality and marketing performance analysis.
Reference to the specific production line used for this batch. Links to equipment master data.
Reference to the production order that authorized this batch manufacturing run. Links to SAP PP production order master.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.routing. Business justification: The batch_record currently stores routing_version as a denormalized STRING. This should be replaced with a proper FK to routing, which contains the authoritative routing version, operation sequence, a
Reference to the finished goods SKU produced in this batch. Links to product master data.
Actual cost per unit incurred for this batch. Includes material, labor, and overhead variances.
Unique manufacturing batch identifier assigned at production initiation. Used for product traceability and recall management per GMP requirements.
Actual production quantity achieved for this batch. Used for yield calculation and inventory reconciliation.
Planned production quantity for this batch as specified in the production order. Measured in base unit of measure.
Current lifecycle status of the batch. Controls inventory availability and distribution eligibility per GMP workflow.
Time in minutes required for line changeover before this batch. Key metric for SMED and lean manufacturing initiatives.
Date and time when this batch record was first created in the system. Audit trail for record lifecycle.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for cost amounts. Supports multi-currency manufacturing operations.
Total unplanned downtime in minutes during batch production. Used for OEE calculation and maintenance planning.
Number of electronic signatures captured during batch record lifecycle. Required for FDA 21 CFR Part 11 compliance.
Date after which the product should not be used or sold. Critical for FEFO inventory management and consumer safety.
The gmp compliant of the batch record record
Number of GMP deviations recorded for this batch. Used for quality trend analysis and CAPA tracking.
Indicates whether any GMP deviations were recorded during batch production. Triggers quality review workflow.
The gmp reviewed of the batch record record
Date and time when this batch record was last updated. Audit trail for GMP compliance and data integrity.
Indicates whether complete raw material lot traceability has been captured for this batch. Required for recall readiness.
The lot number of the batch record record
The manufacture date of the batch record record
The manufactured quantity of the batch record record
Calendar date of batch production. Printed on product packaging for consumer information and regulatory compliance.
Date and time when batch production was completed and final packaging finished. Used for cycle time analysis.
Date and time when batch production was initiated on the shop floor. Captured from MES system.
Overall equipment effectiveness achieved during batch production. Composite metric of availability, performance, and quality.
Indicates whether the batch has been released by Quality Assurance for distribution. Required before inventory availability.
Date and time when Quality Assurance approved the batch for release. Marks transition from quarantine to available inventory.
The quantity produced of the batch record record
Indicates whether this batch is subject to a product recall. Triggers supply chain hold and customer notification.
Date when recall was initiated for this batch. Used for regulatory reporting and recall effectiveness tracking.
Indicates whether this batch record is locked from further edits. Required for GMP record integrity after quality release.
Indicates whether batch is on regulatory hold pending investigation or approval. Prevents distribution until cleared.
The release date of the batch record record
The released of the batch record record
The review by exception of the batch record record
Quantity of product requiring rework during batch production. Indicator of process capability and quality issues.
Quantity of product scrapped during batch production. Used for yield calculation and waste reduction analysis.
Number of days between manufacturing date and expiry date. Derived from product stability testing.
The source system code of the batch record record
Standard cost per unit for this batch based on BOM and routing. Used for cost variance analysis.
Unit of measure for batch size quantities (e.g., KG, LTR, EA, CS). Aligns with GS1 standards.
The uom of the batch record record
The updated timestamp of the batch record record
Reference to the master batch record document stored in Veeva Vault. Links to regulatory document management system.
Ratio of actual batch size to planned batch size expressed as percentage. Key performance indicator for manufacturing efficiency.
Unique identifier for the bill of materials record. Primary key for the manufacturing BOM entity.
The manufacturing facility id of the manufacturing bom record
Reference to the finished good or semi-finished SKU that this BOM defines. Links to the product master for which this material structure applies.
The product bom id of the manufacturing bom record

Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Manufacturing BOM components must conform to quality specifications that drive incoming inspection and supplier qualification. Linking BOM to specification enables automated inspection plan generation

Reference to the preferred or sole-source supplier for this component. Used for procurement planning, supplier performance tracking, and supply chain risk management. Nullable if multiple suppliers are available.

Foreign key linking to sales.trade_account. Business justification: In consumer goods, retailer-specific (private label) BOMs are a standard practice — a BOM may be formulated exclusively for one trade account with unique specifications. Linking manufacturing_bom to t

Indicates whether this component contains or may contain allergens as defined by FDA and EU REACH regulations. True if allergen present. Used for product labeling, consumer safety warnings, and regulatory compliance.

Identifier for a group of interchangeable components that can substitute for each other in production. Used for supply chain flexibility, multi-sourcing strategies, and material availability optimization.

The selection priority for this component within its alternative item group. Lower numbers indicate higher priority. Used by MRP to determine preferred material selection when multiple alternatives are available.
The date when this BOM version was formally approved for production use. Establishes the official release date for regulatory compliance and change control purposes.

The name or identifier of the person who approved this BOM version for production use. Required for GMP compliance and quality assurance. Typically a product development manager, quality manager, or regulatory affairs specialist.

Indicates whether this component is automatically consumed (backflushed) when the finished good is confirmed in the MES system, rather than requiring explicit material issue transactions. True if backflushed. Common for high-volume, low-value components.
The reference quantity of the finished good for which the component quantities are defined. Typically 1 unit, 100 units, or 1 batch. All component quantities are expressed per this base quantity.
The unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter, BOX for box). Must align with the SKU primary UOM.
The base uom of the manufacturing bom record
The externally-known unique business identifier for this BOM, typically assigned by the PLM or ERP system. Used for cross-system reference and production order material requirements.
Current lifecycle status of the BOM. Active BOMs are used for production planning and MRP runs. Inactive or obsolete BOMs are retained for historical reference and regulatory traceability.

Classification of the BOM structure. Production BOM is used for standard manufacturing, co-product for joint production scenarios, phantom for intermediate assemblies not stocked, engineering for R&D prototypes, and planning for demand forecasting.
The date after which this specific component is no longer valid within the BOM structure. Nullable for open-ended components. Used for material phase-out and supplier transition management.
The date from which this specific component becomes valid within the BOM structure. Supports phased material transitions, supplier changes, and cost optimization initiatives.

Classification of the component type. Stock items are inventory-managed materials, non-stock are direct procurement items, phantom are intermediate assemblies not physically stocked, text items are instructions, and variable size items have flexible dimensions.

Sequential line number for each component within the BOM structure. Defines the order of components for display, reporting, and regulatory ingredient listing (descending order by weight for INCI compliance).
The quantity of this component required to produce the base quantity of the finished good. Used for MRP explosion, production order material requirements, and COGS calculation.
The unit of measure for the component quantity (e.g., KG for raw materials, EA for packaging units, L for liquids). Must align with the component material master UOM.

The percentage of total product cost attributed to this component. Used for COGS calculation, profitability analysis, and cost optimization initiatives. Sum of all component cost allocations should equal 100% for the BOM.
The date and time when this BOM record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and regulatory compliance documentation per ISO 9001 requirements.

Indicates whether this component is critical for product quality, safety, or regulatory compliance. True if critical. Triggers enhanced quality control, supplier qualification requirements, and inventory safety stock policies.
The date after which this BOM version is no longer valid for new production orders. Nullable for open-ended BOMs. Used for phase-out management and regulatory compliance transitions.
The effective from of the manufacturing bom record
The date from which this BOM version becomes valid for production planning and MRP explosion. Supports planned material transitions and new product launches.
The effective until of the manufacturing bom record

Indicates whether this component is classified as a hazardous material requiring special handling, storage, and disposal per OSHA and EPA regulations. True if hazardous. Triggers safety data sheet requirements and GMP compliance protocols.

business_glossary_term: International Nomenclature of Cosmetic Ingredients (INCI) Name
description: The standardized INCI name for cosmetic ingredients as required by FDA and EU regulations. Used for product labeling, regulatory submissions, and consumer transparency. Mandatory for personal care and cosmetic products.
The date and time when this BOM record was last updated. Tracks formulation changes, component substitutions, and regulatory updates. Critical for change control and GMP compliance.

The procurement or production lead time for this component in days. Used for MRP scheduling, production order release timing, and inventory planning. Includes supplier lead time plus internal processing time.

Defines the lot sizing rule for this component in production orders. Exact requires precise quantity, minimum allows overages, multiple requires quantities in specific increments, none has no restriction. Impacts material planning and waste management.
The manufacturing bom status of the manufacturing bom record

The minimum quantity that must be procured or produced for this component. Driven by supplier MOQ, production batch constraints, or economic order quantity calculations. Nullable if no minimum applies.
External reference identifier linking this BOM to the source PLM system record. Enables traceability to engineering change orders, formulation documents, and R&D specifications.

Free-text field capturing regulatory compliance requirements, restrictions, or special handling instructions for this component. Includes FDA registration numbers, EU REACH compliance status, GMP requirements, and safety data sheet references.

The expected waste or loss percentage for this component during production. Used to adjust gross material requirements in MRP runs. Typical values range from 0% to 10% depending on material type and process complexity.
The source system code of the manufacturing bom record

Certification or standard that this component meets for sustainable sourcing (e.g., FSC for paper, RSPO for palm oil, Fair Trade, Organic). Used for corporate sustainability reporting and consumer transparency initiatives.
The updated timestamp of the manufacturing bom record

Version identifier for the BOM structure. Incremented when material composition changes due to reformulation, cost optimization, or regulatory compliance updates. Supports change control and audit trail.
Unique system-generated identifier for the planned order record. Primary key for the planned order entity.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: S&OP and MRP planning in consumer goods aligns production plans with campaign calendars. Campaign-driven demand spikes (e.g., promotional launches) generate planned orders; linking them enables brand
Reference to the production order that was created from this planned order upon conversion. Null if not yet converted.

Foreign key linking to supply.demand_plan. Business justification: MRP/DRP demand-supply traceability: planned orders are generated from demand plans. Planners need to trace which demand signal triggered each planned order for exception management and demand fulfillm

Foreign key linking to supply.network_node. Business justification: Supply network destination planning: planned orders specify which supply network node will receive the produced goods. Role-prefixed 'destination_' distinguishes from the source manufacturing facility

Foreign key linking to distribution.distribution_facility. Business justification: MRP/IBP planned orders in consumer goods are generated to replenish specific distribution centers. DC replenishment planning, OTIF reporting, and supply network optimization all require knowing which

Foreign key linking to consumer.dtc_order. Business justification: Make-to-Order DTC Demand Traceability: In DTC consumer goods, individual DTC orders trigger make-to-order MRP planned production runs. Linking planned_order to dtc_order enables operations teams to tr

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_bom. Business justification: MRP generates planned orders based on a specific Bill of Materials. The planned_order must reference the manufacturing_bom used to determine component requirements and quantities during planning. This

Foreign key linking to marketing.marketing_brand. Business justification: Brand-level S&OP capacity planning requires aggregating planned production orders by brand without joining through SKU-to-brand hierarchy. Brand managers review planned supply vs. brand-level demand t
The production line id of the planned order record

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.routing. Business justification: MRP planning uses routing master data to calculate lead times, work center capacity requirements, and scheduling windows. The planned_order should reference the routing that defines the production pro

Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: In make-to-order consumer goods operations, MRP/IBP planned orders are directly triggered by sales orders. Linking planned_order to the originating sales order enables demand-driven production plannin
Reference to the SKU (Stock Keeping Unit) that this planned order is for. Links to the product master data.

Foreign key linking to supply.sop_cycle. Business justification: S&OP cycle supply planning: planned_order has sop_cycle_reference (plain text denormalization) confirming this business relationship. Planned orders are generated within S&OP cycles; the FK formalizes
The date when the produced or procured material from this planned order is expected to be available for consumption or shipment.
The date when this planned order was converted to a production order. Null if not yet converted.
Indicates whether this planned order has been converted to a production order: NOT_CONVERTED (still in planning), PARTIALLY_CONVERTED (split conversion), FULLY_CONVERTED (completely converted).
User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that created this planned order.
System timestamp when this planned order record was first created in the system.

Classification of the demand signal that triggered this planned order: FORECAST (demand planning), CUSTOMER_ORDER (firm sales order), STOCK_TRANSFER (inter-plant), SAFETY_STOCK (replenishment), PROMOTION (trade promotion demand).

business_glossary_term: Material Requirements Planning (MRP) Exception Message Code
description: Code representing MRP exception conditions (e.g., EX01 = reschedule in, EX02 = reschedule out, EX03 = cancel, EX10 = expedite). Empty if no exception.
Human-readable description of the MRP exception condition requiring planner attention.
The firmed flag of the planned order record
The date when this planned order was firmed by a planner. Null if not yet firmed.
Boolean flag indicating whether this planned order is firmed (locked from automatic MRP changes). True = firmed, False = subject to MRP rescheduling.
Indicates whether this planned order must comply with GMP regulations. True for pharmaceutical and cosmetic products requiring ISO 22716 compliance.
Version identifier from SAP IBP system that generated the demand signal driving this planned order. Format: IBP-YYYYMM.
User ID or system identifier of the person or automated process that last modified this planned order.
System timestamp when this planned order record was last updated or modified.
Code indicating the lot-sizing procedure used: EX (exact lot size), HB (lot-for-lot), FX (fixed lot size), PD (periodic lot size), WM (weekly lot size).
Code identifying the MRP controller or planning group responsible for this material and plant combination.
Reference to the specific MRP run that generated or last modified this planned order. Links to MRP execution history.
The mrp run date of the planned order record
Free-text field for planners to add comments, special instructions, or contextual information about this planned order.
Business-readable unique identifier for the planned order, typically system-generated following organizational numbering conventions.

Current lifecycle status of the planned order: DRAFT (initial creation), FIRMED (locked from MRP changes), RELEASED (approved for execution), CONVERTED (converted to production order), DELETED (marked for deletion), CANCELLED (cancelled before conversion).

Classification of the planned order origin: MPS (Master Production Schedule), MRP (Material Requirements Planning generated), CBP (Consumption-Based Planning), MANUAL (manually created), FORECAST (forecast-driven), SAFETY (safety stock replenishment).
The planned end date of the planned order record
The planned order number of the planned order record
The quantity of the SKU planned to be produced or procured in this order, expressed in the base unit of measure.
The planned start date of the planned order record
The planning time bucket this order belongs to, expressed as YYYY-Wnn (week), YYYY-Mnn (month), or YYYY-Qn (quarter). Used for MPS and S&OP alignment.
Two-digit code defining the planning strategy (e.g., 10 = make-to-stock, 20 = make-to-order, 40 = planning with final assembly, 50 = planning without final assembly).
Priority level assigned to this planned order for scheduling and resource allocation: LOW, NORMAL, HIGH, URGENT.
Indicates whether this planned order is for in-house production (IN_HOUSE), external procurement (EXTERNAL), or can be either (BOTH).
Identifies the specific combination of BOM (Bill of Materials) and routing to be used for production. Links to production master data.
The inventory level that triggers generation of a planned order for replenishment.
The safety stock level maintained for this SKU at this plant, influencing planned order generation.
Version identifier for the MPS schedule that generated or governs this planned order. Enables tracking of schedule changes over time.
The planned date when production or procurement activities for this order should be completed and goods available.
The planned date when production or procurement activities for this order should begin.
The source system code of the planned order record
The unit of measure for the planned quantity (e.g., EA for each, KG for kilogram, L for liter). Follows ISO standard unit codes.
The uom of the planned order record
The updated timestamp of the planned order record
Unique identifier for the production confirmation record. Primary key for shop floor execution tracking.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.batch_record. Business justification: A production confirmation is a shop floor execution event that contributes to the GMP batch record. Each confirmation (operation-level execution) rolls up into a batch_record (the complete eBMR for th
Reference to the cost center to which labor and overhead costs for this operation are allocated for financial accounting.
Reference to the manufacturing plant or facility where this operation was performed.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.production_line. Business justification: A production confirmation captures shop floor execution at a specific production line. While work_center_id provides granular equipment-level context, production_line_id is needed for line-level OEE r
Reference to the parent production order under which this operation was executed.
The routing id of the production confirmation record
Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Production confirmation reports must reference the sales order they fulfill for accurate shipping and invoicing.
Reference to the finished or semi-finished material (SKU) produced by this operation.

Foreign key linking to supply.sop_cycle. Business justification: S&OP supply review actuals reconciliation: production confirmations represent actual output that must be reconciled against S&OP cycle supply targets during supply review meetings. This link enables a
Reference to the work center (production line, machine, or station) where this operation was performed.
SAP CO activity type code representing the type of work performed (e.g., machine hours, labor hours, setup hours) for cost allocation purposes.
Actual date and time when the operation was completed and confirmed by the operator. Used for OEE calculation and production order settlement.
Actual operator labor time spent on this operation including manual work, monitoring, and quality checks. Measured in minutes.
Actual time the machine or equipment was actively running during this operation. Excludes setup and downtime. Measured in minutes.
Actual time spent on machine setup, changeover, and preparation activities before production began. Measured in minutes.
Actual date and time when the operator began executing this operation on the shop floor. Captured from MES or manual confirmation.
Manufacturing batch or lot number assigned to the output produced in this operation. Critical for traceability and recall management.
Free-text notes or comments entered by the operator or supervisor regarding this operation execution, including any issues or observations.
The confirmation number of the production confirmation record
System timestamp when this confirmation record was created and submitted to the ERP or MES system.
Classification of the confirmation event indicating whether this is a partial progress update, final completion, milestone checkpoint, rework confirmation, or cancellation.
The confirmed quantity of the production confirmation record
Actual quantity of defective or waste material generated during this operation. Used for yield variance analysis and quality tracking.
Actual quantity of good output produced and confirmed for this operation. Measured in the base unit of measure for the material.
The created timestamp of the production confirmation record
Total unplanned downtime during this operation due to equipment failure, material shortage, or other interruptions. Measured in minutes.
Indicates whether this operation was executed in compliance with GMP standards as required for regulated consumer goods products.
The labor hours of the production confirmation record
System timestamp when this confirmation record was last updated or modified. Used for change tracking and audit purposes.
The machine hours of the production confirmation record
Unique transaction reference from the Siemens Opcenter MES system that originated this confirmation. Used for traceability and system reconciliation.
Calculated availability component of OEE for this operation, representing the percentage of scheduled time that equipment was available for production.
Calculated performance component of OEE for this operation, representing actual production speed versus ideal speed.
Calculated quality component of OEE for this operation, representing the percentage of good output versus total output produced.
Sequential operation identifier within the production order routing. Typically a 4-digit numeric code representing the step in the manufacturing process.
Current lifecycle status of the manufacturing operation indicating its execution state in the production workflow.
Financial posting date when this confirmation was recorded in the ERP system for inventory and cost accounting purposes.
Indicates whether this operation output requires quality inspection before release to inventory or next operation.
Indicates whether this confirmation has been reversed or cancelled due to error correction or process adjustment.
Standardized code explaining why this confirmation was reversed, if applicable. Used for process improvement and error tracking.
Indicates whether this confirmation represents a rework operation to correct defects from a previous production run.
Planned completion date and time for this operation as determined by production scheduling or MES system.
Planned start date and time for this operation as determined by production scheduling or MES system.
The scrap quantity of the production confirmation record
Production shift identifier during which this operation was executed (e.g., SHIFT_1, DAY, NIGHT). Used for shift performance analysis.
Identifier of the originating system that created this confirmation record (SAP PP, Siemens Opcenter MES, or manual entry).
Standard unit of measure for the confirmed quantities (e.g., KG for kilograms, EA for each, LT for liters). Aligns with material master UOM.
The uom of the production confirmation record
The updated timestamp of the production confirmation record
Standardized code explaining any significant variance between planned and actual quantities or times. Used for root cause analysis.
Business identifier for the work order assigned to this manufacturing operation. Externally visible reference used across shop floor systems.
The yield quantity of the production confirmation record
Unique identifier for the production order. Primary key for the production order entity.

Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Consumer goods companies create campaign-specific production orders for promotional packs and limited-edition SKUs. Linking production_order to campaign enables campaign COGS calculation, marketing RO

Foreign key linking to finance.company_code. Business justification: Production orders are created within a company code for legal entity financial reporting. In multi-entity consumer goods companies with contract or intercompany manufacturing, company code on producti
Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Cost accounting report requires each production order to be charged to a finance cost center for OPEX allocation.

Foreign key linking to supply.demand_plan. Business justification: S&OP/IBP demand fulfillment traceability: production orders are created to fulfill demand plans. Planners require this link for demand-supply variance analysis and S&OP cycle supply review reporting.

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: Production orders trigger an inspection plan; linking allows the order execution system to retrieve the exact inspection plan governing sampling and testing for that batch.

Foreign key linking to manufacturing.manufacturing_bom. Business justification: Production order consumes a specific manufacturing BOM; adding manufacturing_bom_id FK connects BOM to order and eliminates isolation of manufacturing_bom.
The manufacturing facility id of the production order record
Foreign key linking to marketing.brand. Business justification: Production Planning Dashboard ties orders to brands to enable demand forecasting and coordinated marketing launch planning.
FK to manufacturing.production_line

Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Production orders settle actual manufacturing costs and variances to profit centers for brand/segment P&L reporting. In consumer goods SAP CO, production order settlement to profit center is a standar
Foreign key linking to manufacturing.routing. Business justification: Each production order follows a routing; adding routing_id FK links routing to order and eliminates isolation of routing.

Foreign key linking to sales.order. Business justification: Order fulfillment process requires linking each production order to the originating sales order to track manufacturing against sales commitments.
Reference to the SKU being produced in this production order. Links to the product master data.

Foreign key linking to supply.sop_cycle. Business justification: S&OP cycle production performance reporting: production orders are released within a specific S&OP cycle. This link enables actual vs planned production volume reconciliation at cycle level — a standa
Foreign key linking to sales.trade_account. Business justification: Required for Production Order to Customer Account linkage for OTIF SLA reporting and order fulfillment traceability.
Actual cost incurred for the production order. Includes material, labor, and overhead costs. Used for variance analysis.
The actual end of the production order record
The actual end date of the production order record
Actual date and time when production execution completed. Used for OEE calculation and performance analysis.
The actual start of the production order record
The actual start date of the production order record
Actual date and time when production execution began on the shop floor. Captured from MES system.
Unique batch or lot number assigned to the production run. Critical for traceability and recall management per GMP requirements.
Time in minutes required for line changeover before starting this production order. Used for scheduling and efficiency analysis.
Actual quantity produced and confirmed as good output. Represents yield after quality inspection.
Cost object (production order number or process order) used for collecting actual costs. Links to CO-PC product costing.
Date and time when the production order was created in the system. First event in order lifecycle.
Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for cost amounts. Typically company code currency.
Total unplanned downtime in minutes during production execution. Impacts OEE availability component.
Indicates whether this production order is subject to GMP compliance requirements per ISO 22716 standards. True for regulated products.
Inspection lot number created for quality control of the production output. Links to QM inspection results.
Date and time of the most recent update to the production order record. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.
SAP material number for the finished good being produced. Business identifier for the product in ERP system.
Three-character code identifying the MRP controller responsible for planning this material. Links to planner master data.
Free-text notes or comments related to the production order. Captures special instructions, issues, or observations from shop floor.
Overall Equipment Effectiveness percentage for this production run. Calculated as Availability × Performance × Quality. Industry standard KPI.
Business identifier for the production order. Externally-known unique order number assigned by SAP S/4HANA PP system.
Planned quantity to be produced in base unit of measure. Target output for the production order.

Current lifecycle status of the production order. CRTD=Created, REL=Released, PCNF=Partially Confirmed, TECO=Technically Complete, CNF=Confirmed, DLV=Delivered, CLSD=Closed. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: CRTD|REL|PCNF|TECO|CNF|DLV|CLSD — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
Classification of production order. PP01=Standard Production, PP03=Rework, PP05=Co-Product, PP10=Process Order, PP20=Batch Order.
Standard or planned cost for producing the order quantity. Calculated from BOM and routing costs.
The planned quantity of the production order record
Four-character code identifying the manufacturing facility where production occurs. Maps to SAP plant master.
Priority level for production scheduling. 1=Highest priority (rush/expedite), 5=Lowest priority (standard). Used for sequencing and resource allocation.
Name of the production supervisor responsible for overseeing execution of this order on the shop floor.
Production version defining the specific BOM and routing combination used. Enables multiple production methods for same material.
Indicates whether quality inspection is mandatory before goods receipt. Drives QM integration and inspection lot creation.
User ID of the person who released the production order for execution. Audit trail for production authorization.
Date and time when the production order was released for shop floor execution. Triggers material availability check and capacity reservation.
The scheduled end of the production order record
The scheduled end date of the production order record
Planned date when production is scheduled to complete. Used for capacity planning and delivery commitments.
The scheduled start of the production order record
Planned date when production is scheduled to begin. Set during production planning and MRP run.
Quantity of material scrapped or rejected during production. Used for yield analysis and cost accounting.
Rule for settling production order costs. FUL=Full settlement, PAR=Partial settlement, PER=Periodic settlement.
The source system code of the production order record
Base unit of measure for quantities. Standard ISO codes such as EA (each), KG (kilogram), L (liter), M (meter).
The uom of the production order record
The updated timestamp of the production order record
Actual yield percentage calculated as (confirmed_quantity / order_quantity) * 100. Key performance metric for production efficiency.
User ID of the person who created the production order in the system. Audit trail for order origination.
Unique identifier for the production routing master record. Primary key.

Foreign key linking to quality.inspection_plan. Business justification: In consumer goods GMP manufacturing, inspection plans are assigned to routing task lists to define quality gates at specific operations. This master data relationship (SAP QM task list assignment) ens
The manufacturing facility id of the routing record
Reference to the Bill of Materials (BOM) that defines the raw materials and components consumed by this routing. Links routing to material requirements.
Reference to the finished or semi-finished SKU that this routing defines the manufacturing process for.

Foreign key linking to quality.specification. Business justification: Routing operations in GMP consumer goods manufacturing have associated process specifications (temperature limits, mixing time, pressure ranges) that define critical process parameters. Linking routin
The work center id of the routing record
Approval status of the routing for production use. Approved routings are validated and authorized; pending routings await review; rejected routings require rework.
Date and time when this routing was approved for production use. Supports audit trail and compliance requirements.
Standard production lot size for which the routing operation times and resource requirements are defined. Used as the basis for scaling production schedules.
Unit of measure for the base quantity (e.g., KG, L, EA, M). Aligns with GS1 unit codes.
Time required to transition production lines from the previous product to this routing. Critical for production scheduling and minimizing downtime.
ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the standard cost amount.
Date and time when this routing record was first created in the system. Supports audit trail and data lineage.
Detailed textual description of the routing, including its purpose, scope, and any special instructions for production planners and shop floor operators.
The effective from of the routing record
The effective until of the routing record
Overall expected scrap percentage for the routing, representing anticipated waste or defects. Used for material planning and cost estimation.
Overall expected yield percentage for the routing, representing the ratio of good output to total input. Used for production planning and variance analysis.
Indicates whether this routing includes GMP-critical operations requiring enhanced documentation, validation, and compliance controls per ISO 22716 and FDA regulations.
Number of in-process control checkpoints defined in this routing. IPC checkpoints are quality inspection points during production per ISO 22716 and GMP requirements.
Date and time when this routing record was last modified. Supports audit trail and change tracking.

Constraint on production lot sizes for this routing. Fixed requires exact base quantity; minimum/maximum define bounds; multiple requires lot sizes to be multiples of base quantity; none allows any lot size.
Maximum production lot size allowed for this routing. Nullable if no maximum constraint exists.
Minimum production lot size allowed for this routing. Nullable if no minimum constraint exists.
The operation count of the routing record
The operation description of the routing record
The operation number of the routing record

JSON array or delimited string capturing the complete sequence of operation steps. Each operation includes: operation_number, work_center_code, operation_description, control_key (internal_processing|external_processing|milestone), setup_time_minutes, machine_time_minutes, labor_time_minutes, yield_quantity, scrap_percentage, gmp_critical_step_flag, ipc_checkpoint_flag. Unique surrogate identifier for each consumer address record in the Consumer Goods DTC/B2C platform. Primary key for the consumer_address data product.
The household id of the address record
Reference to the consumer/shopper profile this address belongs to. Links the address record to the master consumer party in the CRM and DTC systems.

Current lifecycle status of the address record. Active addresses are eligible for order fulfillment and marketing. Inactive addresses are retained for historical reporting. Deleted status supports GDPR right-to-erasure soft-delete workflows.

Classification of the address by its business purpose. Billing addresses are used for invoicing and financial compliance; shipping addresses drive DTC order fulfillment and last-mile logistics; home and work addresses support personalized marketing and consumer segmentation.
The amount of the address record

U.S. Census Bureau census tract code for the address location. Enables demographic overlay analysis, market penetration reporting by socioeconomic segment, and trade area planning for consumer goods distribution.
City or municipality of the consumer's address. Used for geographic segmentation, regional marketing campaigns, distribution zone assignment, and regulatory data residency tracking.
The address code of the address record

Indicates whether explicit consumer consent was obtained for storing and processing this address record under applicable privacy regulations (GDPR/CCPA). True indicates consent is on file; False requires consent collection before marketing use.
Date on which the consumer provided consent for storage and processing of this address record. Required for GDPR Article 7 compliance documentation and CCPA opt-in audit trails.

Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the consumer's address (e.g., USA, GBR, DEU). Drives GDPR/CCPA data residency compliance, international shipping eligibility, tax jurisdiction, and regulatory reporting.

Date and time when this address record was first created in the system. Supports GDPR Article 30 records of processing activities, data lineage tracking, and audit trail requirements for consumer data governance.
The currency code of the address record
Governance/lineage metadata added in v2 rebuild for ECM/MVM completeness.

Geographic data residency classification for this address record, indicating the regulatory jurisdiction governing data storage and processing. Drives GDPR (EU), CCPA (US/CA), and other regional privacy law compliance for data localization requirements.

Numeric score (0.00–100.00) representing the likelihood that a physical shipment or direct mail piece will be successfully delivered to this address. Derived from address validation provider output. Supports carrier selection and DTC fulfillment risk management.
The address description of the address record

Nielsen Designated Market Area (DMA) code for the address location. Used for media planning, Share of Voice (SOV) analysis, and regional brand marketing investment allocation in consumer goods trade promotion programs.

Indicates whether the consumer has opted out of physical direct mail marketing to this address. When True, this address must be suppressed from direct mail campaign execution lists. Supports GDPR right-to-object and CCPA opt-out compliance.

USPS Delivery Point Validation confirmation code indicating the deliverability of the address. Y=Confirmed deliverable, S=Confirmed deliverable (secondary info missing), D=Confirmed deliverable (secondary info present but unneeded), N=Not confirmed. Critical for DTC fulfillment and direct mail campaigns.

The date from which this address record is considered valid and active for business use. Supports bi-temporal data modeling, address history tracking, and GDPR right-to-erasure compliance by enabling point-in-time address reconstruction.
The effective from of the address record
The effective until of the address record

The date after which this address record is no longer valid for business use. Null indicates an open-ended active address. Used for address lifecycle management, historical reporting, and GDPR data minimization compliance.

Classification of the address format to guide formatting, validation, and carrier routing logic. PO Box addresses may not be eligible for certain DTC carriers; military addresses (APO/FPO/DPO) require specific handling; rural route addresses may have extended delivery windows.

Precision level of the geocoded latitude/longitude coordinates for this address. Rooftop indicates exact parcel-level geocoding; range_interpolated indicates interpolation between street numbers; geometric_center indicates centroid of a polygon. Impacts reliability of proximity-based analytics.
Governance/lineage metadata added in v2 rebuild for ECM/MVM completeness.
Indicates whether this address is the consumer's default billing address for payment processing and invoice generation. Used in DTC checkout flows and financial compliance reporting.

Indicates whether this address is the consumer's default shipping destination for DTC orders. Distinct from is_primary as a consumer may have a primary home address but a different default shipping address (e.g., workplace).

Indicates whether this is the consumer's primary address record for the given address type. When True, this address is used as the default for order fulfillment, marketing communications, and regulatory correspondence.
The is validated of the address record
Geographic latitude coordinate of the consumer's address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Enables geocoded proximity analysis, delivery zone optimization, and location-based personalized marketing.

Primary street address line including house/building number and street name. Core component of the consumer's physical location used for DTC order fulfillment, direct mail marketing, and GDPR/CCPA data residency compliance.
Secondary address line for apartment, suite, unit, floor, or building designations. Supplements address_line_1 to ensure complete and accurate delivery routing for DTC shipments.
Tertiary address line for additional location details such as neighborhood, district, or care-of designations. Used in international address formats where three address lines are required.

Geographic longitude coordinate of the consumer's address in decimal degrees (WGS84 datum). Used alongside latitude for geocoded proximity analysis, store locator features, and last-mile delivery optimization.
The address name of the address record
The notes of the address record

The four-digit USPS ZIP+4 extension code that narrows delivery to a specific block face or building. Enables postal presort discounts for direct mail campaigns and improves delivery precision for DTC shipments within the United States.
ZIP or postal code of the consumer's address. Used for last-mile delivery routing, trade area analysis, demographic segmentation, and carrier rate determination in DTC fulfillment.
The quantity of the address record
The record source system of the address record
Classification of the address as residential or commercial. Impacts carrier surcharge calculations (UPS/FedEx residential delivery fees), marketing segmentation, and DTC fulfillment cost modeling.

The originating channel or system through which this address was captured. Supports data lineage, source quality scoring, and GDPR/CCPA consent audit trails. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crm|dtc_checkout|loyalty_enrollment|call_center|mobile_app|web_portal|third_party|manual_entry — promote to reference product]
Conformed governance attribute source_record_id.

The native record identifier from the originating operational system of record (e.g., Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud Contact Address ID, SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Address GUID). Enables cross-system reconciliation and data lineage tracing in the Silver Layer.

The full address formatted as a single standardized string per USPS/UPU postal standards after address validation and normalization (e.g., '123 MAIN ST APT 4B, CHICAGO IL 60601-1234'). Used as the canonical address for carrier label generation and deduplication.

State, province, or administrative region of the consumer's address using ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes (e.g., US-CA, CA-ON). Supports tax jurisdiction determination, regional compliance, and territory-based sales reporting.

IANA time zone identifier for the address location (e.g., America/New_York, Europe/London). Used for scheduling personalized marketing communications at optimal local times and for regulatory compliance with time-sensitive consumer notifications.
The uom of the address record

Date and time when this address record was last modified. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks Silver Layer pipeline, data freshness monitoring, and GDPR data accuracy compliance tracking.

Governance/lineage metadata added in v2 rebuild for ECM/MVM completeness.

Name of the third-party address validation service used to verify this address (e.g., USPS CASS, SmartyStreets, Loqate, Google Maps API). Supports audit trails for data quality governance and vendor performance tracking.

Current validation state of the address record as determined by address verification services (e.g., USPS CASS, SmartyStreets, Loqate). Validated addresses reduce failed DTC deliveries and return-to-sender costs. Failed addresses require consumer outreach before order fulfillment.

Date and time when the address was last validated by the address verification service. Used to determine if re-validation is needed based on data freshness policies and to support data quality SLA reporting.

Unique surrogate identifier for each consent record in the Consumer Goods consumer privacy system. Primary key for the consent_record data product, serving as the SSOT for GDPR Article 7 and CCPA compliance audits.

Reference to the marketing or re-consent campaign that prompted this consent record creation or update. Enables campaign-level consent conversion tracking and supports Trade Promotion Management (TPM) and consumer engagement analytics.

Reference to the Consumer Goods brand for which this consent was captured. Enables brand-level consent reporting and ensures that consent granted for one brand is not incorrectly applied to another brand within the same corporate portfolio.
Reference to the consumer profile record for whom this consent record applies. Links the consent record to the consumer master in the CRM/DTC system (Salesforce Consumer Goods Cloud).
The amount of the consent record record

Channel or mechanism through which the consumer's consent was captured. Identifies whether consent was obtained via a web form, mobile app, in-store interaction, call center agent, paper form, or email link. Required for GDPR proof-of-consent obligations.

Exact date and time (with timezone offset) when the consumer's consent action was recorded in the system. This is the principal business event timestamp for the consent record and is immutable once set. Required for GDPR Article 7 audit trail.

The business reason or event that triggered the most recent change to the consent status. Distinguishes consumer-initiated changes from system-driven expirations, administrative overrides, legal requests (e.g., right to erasure), or re-consent campaigns.
The channel of the consent record record
The consent record code of the consent record record
The consent date of the consent record record
The consent given of the consent record record

ISO 639-1 language code (optionally with ISO 3166-1 country subtag) of the language in which the consent notice was presented to the consumer (e.g., 'en', 'fr', 'de', 'es-MX'). Required to confirm consent was given in an intelligible form per GDPR Article 7.

Reference identifier or URL pointing to the stored proof-of-consent artifact (e.g., screenshot, signed form scan, call recording reference, Veeva Vault document ID). Supports GDPR Article 7(1) obligation to demonstrate that consent was given.
The consent record status of the consent record record

Defines the breadth of the consent grant — whether it applies globally across all Consumer Goods brands and products, is restricted to a specific brand, product category, or a single campaign. Enables granular consent management across the product portfolio.

Current lifecycle status of the consent record indicating whether the consumer has actively granted, withdrawn, or is pending confirmation of consent, or whether the consent has expired past its validity period.

Verbatim text or reference hash of the consent notice, privacy policy excerpt, or opt-in language presented to the consumer at the time of consent capture. Immutable record of what the consumer agreed to, required for GDPR Article 7 proof-of-consent.
The consent timestamp of the consent record record

Category of consent being captured, defining the specific processing activity for which the consumer has provided or withheld consent. Covers marketing email, SMS, push notification, data sharing, profiling, and cookies as required by GDPR and CCPA.

Version identifier of the consent notice, privacy policy, or terms document presented to the consumer at the time of consent capture. Enables tracking of which policy version was in effect when consent was given, critical for GDPR Article 7 compliance.

Comma-separated list of cookie categories for which the consumer has granted consent (e.g., 'functional,analytics,marketing'). Captures granular cookie preferences as required by the ePrivacy Directive and GDPR for digital channels. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: necessary|functional|analytics|marketing|social_media|personalization|advertising — promote to reference product]

ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the consumer's country of residence at the time of consent capture. Determines applicable privacy regulation (e.g., GDPR for EU countries, CCPA for USA/California). Critical for multi-jurisdiction compliance management.

Timestamp when this consent record was first created in the Consumer Goods data platform (Silver Layer). Distinct from capture_timestamp which records the business event time. Supports data lineage and audit trail requirements.
The currency code of the consent record record
The consent record description of the consent record record
Type of device used by the consumer when providing consent in digital channels. Supports forensic audit trails and UX analysis of consent capture flows.

Indicates whether the consumer completed a double opt-in confirmation process (e.g., clicked a confirmation link in a verification email) in addition to the initial consent action. Double opt-in provides stronger proof of consent for marketing communications under GDPR.

Exact date and time when the consumer completed the double opt-in confirmation step. Null if double opt-in was not required or not yet completed. Provides the definitive timestamp for consent activation in double opt-in workflows.

Date from which this consent record is considered active and binding. Typically aligns with the capture date but may differ for backdated or scheduled consent activations. Supports MASTER_AGREEMENT role effective-period tracking.
The effective until of the consent record record

Date on which this consent record expires and must be renewed or re-confirmed by the consumer. Null indicates open-ended consent with no defined expiry. Used to trigger consent renewal workflows and ensure ongoing compliance with GDPR's requirement for time-limited consent.

IP address of the device from which the consumer submitted their consent. Captured for digital consent channels (web form, mobile app, email link) as evidence of consent origin. May be considered PII under GDPR in some EU jurisdictions.

Indicates whether this row represents the current active consent record for the consumer-consent_type combination. Supports Slowly Changing Dimension (SCD Type 2) pattern in the Silver Layer, where historical consent versions are retained for audit purposes and this flag identifies the latest version.

The lawful basis under GDPR Article 6 or equivalent regulation for processing the consumer's personal data. Consent is one of six legal bases; this field distinguishes consent-based processing from other lawful bases to support compliance reporting.

Age threshold (in years) used to determine whether parental consent was required for this consent record, based on the applicable jurisdiction's rules (e.g., 16 for GDPR default, 13 for COPPA, 13-16 for EU member state variations). Null if consumer is not a minor.
The consent record name of the consent record record
The notes of the consent record record

Indicates whether parental or guardian consent was obtained for a minor consumer. Required under GDPR Article 8 for children under 16 (or lower age threshold set by member state) and COPPA for US consumers under 13.

The consent status immediately before the most recent status change. Enables point-in-time audit reconstruction and supports the immutable event log requirement for GDPR Article 7 compliance audits without requiring a separate history table join.

Identifier of the system, application, or human agent (call center representative ID or system name) that processed and recorded the consent event. Supports accountability requirements under GDPR Article 5(2) and provides an audit trail of who or what captured the consent.

business_glossary_term: Profiling and Automated Decision-Making Consent Flag
description: Indicates whether the consumer has consented to automated profiling and decision-making activities, including personalization algorithms, NPS scoring, CLTV modeling, and segmentation. Required under GDPR Article 22 for automated decision-making with significant effects.
The quantity of the consent record record

Date by which the consumer must re-confirm their consent before the current consent record is automatically withdrawn or expired. Null if re-consent is not required. Used to drive automated consent renewal campaigns.

Indicates whether this consent record requires re-confirmation from the consumer due to a material change in the privacy policy, consent notice, or processing purpose. Triggers re-consent outreach workflows in the CRM system.
The record source system of the consent record record

Sequential version number of this consent record for the consumer-consent_type combination. Increments with each status change or update, enabling full audit history reconstruction. Version 1 is the initial consent capture; subsequent versions reflect changes.

The applicable privacy regulation or jurisdiction framework under which this consent record was created and must be managed. Determines the legal basis, retention rules, and consumer rights applicable to this record (e.g., GDPR for EU consumers, CCPA for California residents).

Number of days this consent record must be retained for compliance purposes after the consent is withdrawn or expires. Determined by applicable regulatory requirements (e.g., GDPR Article 5(1)(e) storage limitation, CCPA). Drives automated data lifecycle management.
The source channel of the consent record record
The source system code of the consent record record

Indicates whether the consumer has explicitly consented to their personal data being shared with third-party partners (e.g., retail partners, data brokers, analytics providers). Distinct from general data sharing consent; specifically governs external data transfers.
The uom of the consent record record

Timestamp of the most recent modification to this consent record in the data platform. Used for incremental data processing, change data capture (CDC), and audit trail maintenance in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver Layer.

Channel or mechanism through which the consumer withdrew their consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. Required to demonstrate that withdrawal was as easy as giving consent per GDPR Article 7(3).

Exact date and time when the consumer withdrew their consent. Null if consent has not been withdrawn. GDPR Article 7(3) requires that withdrawal be as easy as giving consent; this timestamp documents the withdrawal event for audit purposes.
Unique surrogate identifier for the household record in the Consumer Goods lakehouse. Primary key for all household-level analytics, loyalty pooling, and basket analysis.

Foreign key linking to marketing.consumer_segment. Business justification: Household-level segmentation is standard in consumer goods for trade promotion targeting, media planning, and panel analysis. The existing market_segment text field is a denormalized segment label; re
Reference to the source household record that was merged into this household during deduplication or household consolidation. Null if no merge has occurred. Supports data lineage and audit trails.
Reference to the designated primary shopper or head-of-household consumer profile. Used to anchor loyalty program pooling and personalization targeting.
The address summary of the household record
The amount of the household record

Indicates whether the household has demonstrated strong brand loyalty to the company's portfolio based on repeat purchase patterns and SOV (Share of Voice) analysis. Used for brand marketing investment decisions.

Indicates whether the household has exercised the right to opt out of the sale of personal information under CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act). Mandatory compliance field for California-resident households.
Indicates whether children under 18 are present in the household. Key trigger for cross-sell targeting of baby, child health, and family-oriented CPG/FMCG product categories.

Categorical CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) band assigned to the household based on historical purchase behavior and predictive modeling. Used for investment prioritization in trade promotion and personalization.
Externally-known alphanumeric business identifier for the household, used in CRM, loyalty, and DTC channel communications. Distinct from the surrogate primary key.
Timestamp of the most recent update to any consent or privacy preference for the household. Critical for GDPR/CCPA audit trails and demonstrating lawful basis for data processing.
ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code representing the country of the household's primary residence. Used for regulatory compliance (GDPR, CCPA) and international market segmentation.
Timestamp when the household record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage, audit trails, and GDPR/CCPA compliance reporting.
The currency code of the household record

Originating system or channel from which the household record was first created or last enriched. Used for data lineage, quality scoring, and source system reconciliation in the lakehouse. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: crm|loyalty_program|ecommerce|panel|pos|dtc|manual — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]
The household description of the household record

Indicates whether the household actively engages with the brand through digital channels (e-commerce, mobile app, social media). Used for DTC (Direct to Consumer) channel strategy and omnichannel personalization.
Date on which the household was dissolved, merged, or deactivated. Null for active households. Used for lifecycle reporting and GDPR/CCPA data retention compliance.
Type of residential dwelling occupied by the household. Used for product category targeting (e.g., home care, cleaning products) and basket size normalization in CPG analytics.
The effective from of the household record
The effective until of the household record
Estimated number of individuals residing in the household. Used for basket size normalization, per-capita consumption analysis, and product volume forecasting in S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning).
Date on which the household record was first established in the system, representing when the household unit was recognized as a distinct entity for loyalty and analytics purposes.

Indicates whether the household has provided valid consent for data processing under GDPR (General Data Protection Regulation). Mandatory for EU-resident households before any marketing or analytics processing.
Broad geographic region of the household's residence (e.g., Northeast, Southeast, Midwest, West, Southwest). Used for regional demand planning and distribution channel management.
The has children of the household record
Current lifecycle state of the household record. 'merged' indicates consolidation with another household; 'dissolved' indicates the household no longer exists as a unit.

Categorical classification of the household composition used for segmentation and cross-sell targeting in CPG/FMCG analytics. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: single|couple|family|multi_generational|shared_living|other — promote to reference product if values expand]

Estimated annual household income bracket used for consumer segmentation, RSP (Recommended Selling Price) sensitivity analysis, and premium product targeting. Derived from third-party panel data or self-reported.
The income bracket of the household record
Timestamp of the most recent modification to the household record. Used for change data capture (CDC) in the Databricks lakehouse Silver layer and data freshness monitoring.

Life stage classification of the household used for targeted marketing and NPD (New Product Development) insights (e.g., young_single, young_couple_no_children, family_with_young_children, family_with_teens, empty_nester, retired). Enables cross-sell triggers such as recommending baby products when life stage transitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: young_single|young_couple_no_children|family_with_young_children|family_with_teens|empty_nester|retired|other — promote to reference product]
The lifecycle stage of the household record
Date on which the household was enrolled in the loyalty program. Used to calculate loyalty tenure, tier progression timelines, and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) cohort analysis.
Current unredeemed loyalty points balance accumulated at the household level across all member consumers. Supports household-level pooling for CPG/FMCG loyalty programs.
Current loyalty program tier of the household based on cumulative purchase activity. Drives reward multipliers, exclusive offers, and CLTV (Customer Lifetime Value) segmentation.

Indicates whether the household has opted in to receive marketing communications (email, SMS, push notifications) from the brand. Governs eligibility for trade promotion and consumer engagement campaigns.
Human-readable label for the household, typically the primary shopper's surname or a family name used for display in CRM and loyalty program communications.
The notes of the household record

Most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) recorded for the household, ranging from 0 to 10. Used to classify households as Promoters (9-10), Passives (7-8), or Detractors (0-6) for consumer engagement programs.
Date on which the most recent NPS (Net Promoter Score) survey response was recorded for the household. Used to assess recency of satisfaction data for consumer engagement decisions.
The number of children of the household record

Indicates whether the household is an active member of a consumer research panel (e.g., Nielsen IQ Consumer Panel). Panel households provide purchase diary data used for market intelligence and SOM (Share of Market) analysis.
Indicates whether the household owns pets. Used for targeted marketing of pet care product categories and cross-sell recommendations within the CPG/FMCG portfolio.
Postal or ZIP code of the household's primary residence. Used for geographic clustering, DRP (Distribution Requirements Planning), and localized trade promotion targeting.

BCP 47 language tag representing the household's preferred communication language (e.g., en-US, es-MX, fr-FR). Used for localized marketing content, product labeling compliance, and consumer engagement personalization.
The dominant channel through which the household makes purchases. Used for DTC (Direct to Consumer) strategy, channel mix analysis, and trade promotion targeting.

The retail banner or chain where the household conducts the majority of its grocery or consumer goods shopping (e.g., Walmart, Target, Kroger). Used for OSA (On Shelf Availability) and POG (Planogram) analytics.

Indicates whether the household regularly purchases private label or store-brand products in addition to or instead of branded CPG products. Used for competitive analysis and brand switching risk assessment.

Categorical band representing how frequently the household makes purchases across CPG/FMCG categories. Derived from POS (Point of Sale) transaction history and used for demand sensing and replenishment planning.
The quantity of the household record
The record source system of the household record
The region of the household record
The size of the household record

Indicates whether the household owns or rents their residence. Used for home improvement and home care product category targeting and consumer segmentation.
